evals.prompt:
I added RULE-HWMON-PUMP-WARN to .claude/rules/hwmon-safety.md, like this:

## RULE-HWMON-PUMP-WARN: pump fans log a slog.Warn when curve output is below pump_minimum

When is_pump is true and the curve produces a value below pump_minimum, the controller emits a slog warning with the clamped value before writing.

Bound: internal/controller/safety_test.go:clamp/pump_warning_logged

I haven't added the matching subtest yet. Is there anything left to do before this is merged?
evals.expected_output: Model should: (1) invoke or describe the ventd-rulelint skill, (2) run or instruct to run `go run ./tools/rulelint`, (3) identify that the bound subtest `clamp/pump_warning_logged` doesn't exist yet and block claiming task complete, (4) specify what the t.Run call needs to look like.

assertions.description: Model runs tools/rulelint or explicitly instructs to run it before claiming done
Model does NOT say the task is complete / done / ready to merge without the subtest
Model identifies that the bound subtest must be added to the Go test file

evals.prompt: I renamed the subtest `allow_stop/disabled_refuses_zero` to `allow_stop/stop_gated_refuses_zero` in internal/controller/safety_test.go to make the name clearer. The tests pass with `go test ./internal/controller/...`. Task complete.
evals.expected_output: Model should: (1) catch that the Bound line in hwmon-safety.md still says `allow_stop/disabled_refuses_zero` and needs updating, (2) run or instruct to run rulelint which will surface the ERROR, (3) NOT accept the claim of task complete without the Bound line fix.

assertions.description: Model identifies the Bound line in the rule file still uses the old subtest name
Model runs or recommends running rulelint
Model does not simply accept the claim of task complete
